subject: Hone Health Q1 2026 investor update
highlights: Crossed $113M ARR in March, up from $86M YoY
Launched women's hormone health vertical (early traction, ~$2M ARR in first quarter)
Hired CFO from Hims (Q1)
risk_flags: Hims & Hers public-market multiple compression (~4-5× revenue) compressing comp valuations
GLP-1 attribution shift — some men's health customers shifting to weight-loss platforms
ask: Looking for warm intros to enterprise health benefits buyers (self-insured employers >5K employees)

subject: Eight Sleep Q1 update — Pod 5 launch
raise_planned: None planned; profitable on a quarterly basis since Q3 2025
highlights: Pod 5 launched March 2026, 18,000 pre-orders in first month
Hardware margin improved from 41% to 49% with manufacturing consolidation
Subscription ARR now 61% of total ARR (up from 48% YoY)
risk_flags: Tariff exposure on China-made components (currently 15%, watching for escalation)
Returns rate on Pod 5 elevated in first 60 days (likely transient)
ask: None this quarter; will share IPO timing thinking in Q3 update

subject: BusRight investor update Q1 2026
raise_planned: Series B, $25-35M, kicking off May 2026 with Bessemer leading conversations
highlights: Crossed $16M ARR, up from $9M YoY
Signed Houston ISD (4th largest district in country) — 5-year contract worth $4.2M
AI route-optimization product live with 80% of base; cuts route count 12-18% on average
risk_flags: Sales cycle remains long (9-14 months for large districts); pipeline coverage at 3.1× target
Encore Technologies (incumbent) cutting prices aggressively on renewals
ask: Intros to school transportation directors in TX, FL, and NY would accelerate FY27 pipeline

subject: On Deck — restructuring update
raise_planned: Bridge round of $3M from existing investors, closing November
highlights: Reduced from $48M Series B post to focus on On Deck Founders as sole product
Cut headcount from 145 to 18 over the past 18 months
Path to default-alive at $5M ARR with current cost structure
risk_flags: Brand and trust impaired post-layoff cycle
Cohort fellowship business never reached previously-projected unit economics
Bridge round priced at 70% discount to last priced round (Series B)
ask: Please consider re-up; bridge is open to existing LPs through Nov 30

subject: Atlys — Series A closed, growth update
raise_planned: Series A closed Jan 2026 — $30M led by Peak XV, $185M post-money
highlights: Crossed $45M ARR in Feb 2026, up from $12M YoY (~280%)
Series A closed at $185M post — strong terms, no liquidation preference creep
Expanded to UK and Singapore destinations (previously US + Schengen only)
risk_flags: Regulatory exposure to changes in US visa processing (election year)
Customer concentration: top 3 destination countries = 78% of volume
ask: Looking for ops talent — VP Operations role open

subject: Yac — shutdown notice and wind-down
raise_planned: Shutdown decision made; no further raise
highlights: Effective Aug 31 2024, Yac ceasing operations
Async voice/video category did not develop as predicted; Loom dominance proved structural
Remaining cash returned to LPs as final distribution (~$0.04 per dollar invested)
risk_flags: Category bet did not work out
Shutdown is orderly; no employee or customer liability
ask: Thanks for the support. Wind-down formalities will close out by Q4 2024.

subject: MarketerHire — closing notice
highlights: Marketplace economics did not converge; CAC remained above LTV through 2023
Attempted enterprise pivot in late 2023 did not gain traction
Company closing with zero distribution to investors
risk_flags: Marketplace category being eaten by Upwork's enterprise tier
